verdict: AI displacement risk: moderate, with 32% of tasks automatable by current frontier models.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, 6, and 7 involve routine cognitive tasks that are highly automatable by current frontier models.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: Task 5 requires creative judgment, but even this task can be partially automated with AI-assisted design tools.

evidence_ledger.contribution: O*NET task list for Graphic Designers

adjacent_careers.name: UI/UX Designer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles involve visual communication and design, but UI/UX designers focus more on user experience.

adjacent_careers.name: Digital Artist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Graphic Designers may use AI-assisted tools to generate artwork, but Digital Artists often work with more abstract concepts.

recent_developments.headline: Adobe Launches New AI-Powered Design Tool
recent_developments.impact: This tool may automate some routine design tasks, but its impact on the role of Graphic Designer is still unclear.

recent_developments.headline: Generative tools enter mainstream creative pipelines
recent_developments.impact: Studios and agencies use AI for ideation, layout, voice, and music drafts — finals still human-finished.

quotes.quote: I am utterly disgusted. This is an insult to life itself.
quotes.speaker: Hayao Miyazaki
quotes.title: Co-founder, Studio Ghibli

quotes.quote: AI is a tool. It will not replace artists. It will replace artists who do not learn to use AI as a tool.
quotes.speaker: Refik Anadol
quotes.title: Media artist

quotes.quote: Generative tools collapse the cost of producing a draft to near zero. The differentiator becomes taste, judgment, and knowing what is worth making at all.
quotes.speaker: John Maeda
quotes.title: Designer; VP of Design and AI, Microsoft
